definitions.text: In or at a place or location (stated, implied or otherwise indicated) that is perceived to be away from, or at a relative distance from, the speaker (compare here).
examples: I know Bristol quite well as I used to live there.
I looked in the cupboard and my keys were there!

definitions.text: At that point, stage, etc., visualised as a distinct place.
examples: He did not stop there, but continued his speech.
They patched up their differences, but matters did not end there.

definitions.text: In a notional place where one will provide support or care; see also be there.
examples: You know that I will always be there for you.

definitions.text: Used to offer encouragement or sympathy; see also there, there and there now.
examples: There, there. Everything is going to turn out all right.

definitions.text: Used to express victory or completion.
That place (previously mentioned or otherwise implied).
examples: We need someone to take us from here to there.
You rinse and de-string the green beans; I'll take it from there.

definitions.text: Used as an expletive subject of be in its sense of “exist”, with the semantic, usually indefinite subject being postponed or (occasionally) implied.
examples: There are two apples on the table. [=Two apples are on the table.]
There is no way to do it. [=No way to do it exists.]

definitions.text: Used with other intransitive verbs of existence, in the same sense, or with other intransitive verbs, adding a sense of existence.
examples: If x is a positive number, then there exists [=there is] a positive number y less than x.
There remain several problems with this approach. [=Several problems remain with this approach.]

definitions.text: Used with other verbs, when raised.
examples: There seems to be some difficulty with the papers. [=It seems that there is some difficulty with the papers.]
I expected there to be a simpler solution. [=I expected that there would be a simpler solution.]
